entries.name: DTF St. Louis
entries.description: Yes, the title means exactly what you think it does, but don’t expect a straightforward sex story from this HBO Max crime comedy. Jason Bateman and David Harbour star as work friends whose mission to spice up their sex lives leads one of them to an untimely death. Written and directed by Steve Conrad (“Wonder,” “Patriot”), the seven-episode miniseries was originally inspired by a true story before finding its feet as a wholly original tale filled with twists, turns, and more than a few kinks. — Adrienne

entries.name: Marshals
entries.description: The sun never sets on the Taylor Sheridan universe, and his latest “Yellowstone” spin-off is shining bright among the most anticipated of the year. Luke Grimes returns as Kayce Dutton, who will step center stage in this series about joining a team of crime-fighting US Marshals. “Yellowstone” fans can’t seem to get enough of this Montana melodrama, and we suspect this new show’s procedural approach will be a big hit for folks hungry for more stories set in this neo-western world. — Adrienne

entries.name: Young Sherlock
entries.description: Nearly 140 years after his debut in “A Study in Scarlet,” the consulting detective shows no sign of waning interest. This month alone gives us at least two different takes on the legendary detective, starting with the Guy Ritchie-directed series “Young Sherlock,” which follows a rough-around-the-edges Sherlock at the beginning of his sleuthing career, solving his first murder at Oxford University and stepping on the path to greatness. Though, if “Young Sherlock” isn't enough to scratch your Holmesian itch, March also kicks off Season 2 of the Sherlock-inspired medical drama “Watson.” — Vanessa

entries.name: Vladimir
entries.description: Rachel Weisz and Leo Woodall are coming to Netflix with a subversive dramedy about a woman’s preoccupation with a younger colleague and the tangled web she creates in his pursuit. Adapted from the book of the same name, Vladimir is a series of firsts for author, creator, and showrunner Julia May Jonas. Not only was the book her debut novel, but also the show marks her first time on a film set (!) and it's as the driving creative force of the miniseries. Expect a cheeky and irreverent eight episodes as Weisz’s unnamed narrator spirals down a rabbit hole of obsession that seems destined to lead to some entertaining plot complications. — Adrienne

entries.name: War Machine
entries.description: It’s all right there in the trailer. Men in camo shooting guns at a big alien robot, possibly on the set of Train Dreams (Australia, actually). Is there anything else to know? Just that the cast and crew are stacked with enough action movie pros to guarantee major onscreen fireworks. The lineup includes star Alan Ritchson of “Reacher” fame, director Patrick Hughes (Expendables 3, The Hitman’s Bodyguard), action staples Jai Courtney (Suicide Squad and Divergent films) and Esai Morales (Mission: Impossible 7 and 8), and many more. — James

entries.name: The Bride!
entries.description: The answer is “yes.” Yes, we need another “Frankenstein” movie already. Director Maggie Gyllenhaal’s take on the Bride of Frankenstein’s story might be the increasingly rare kind of film that gets audiences of all ages to buy movie tickets, from young goths to your parents, who may have been goths too. Have you watched either of the trailers? The romance between the Bride (Jessie Buckley) and Frankenstein’s Monster (Christian Bale) feels like it shares DNA with Joker (the good one) and maybe Bonnie and Clyde? We’re sensing Gyllenhaal has harnessed the spark of life for her second feature as writer-director and is set to deliver the year’s next no-boundaries romance after Wuthering Heights. — Arno

entries.name: Heel
entries.detail: In select theaters and on demand March 6
entries.description: One look at the marketing for Magnolia Pictures’ new movie is all the warning you need to know things are about to get weird. In Heel, a wayward young man’s life of partying is derailed when he’s abducted by a suburban family hell-bent on rehabilitating him, no matter the cost. Originally premiering under the ominous title Good Boy at TIFF in 2025, the twisted thriller (horror movie?) stars Anson Boone, Andrea Riseborough, and Stephen Graham, as he comes off his award-winning run as the creator and star of “Adolescence.” — Adrienne

entries.name: Boyfriend on Demand
entries.description: My quest to consume more swoony, low-stakes romantic comedies kicks off this month with the K-Drama “Boyfriend on Demand.” The 10-episode series stars Blackpink's Jisoo as Seo Mi-rae, a burnt-out artist who escapes the stresses of reality via a dating service that creates a fantastical virtual world full of endless perfect dates. Will she find love outside of the simulation? I mean, absolutely yes, this is a rom-com. But how she gets there? That's going to be the good stuff. — Vanessa

entries.name: One Piece
entries.description: Weigh the anchor (fluff the couch cushions) and hoist the jib (pick up the remote), because it’s time to set sail once again with “One Piece.” Season 2 will see Luffy and the Straw Hats on an adventure to the legendary Grand Line seas in search of the world’s greatest treasure. Be prepared for peculiar new islands and a whole host of enemies, allies, and everything in between. Manga fans, take note! The eight-episode season looks like it will adapt storylines from the Alabasta Saga and feature the Baroque Works crime group, among others. — Adrienne

entries.name: Scarpetta
entries.detail: Series premieres March 11 on Prime Video
entries.description: At least once a year I need a Nicole Kidman goes off moment. The thought of her playing Kay Scarpetta, the medical examiner who lives within 29 Patricia Cornwell novels, makes me hope “Scarpetta” might become a long-running series in the vein of Helen Mirren’s “Prime Suspect” (which I’ve been rewatching lately). Season 1 finds Scarpetta leaning into her forensic pathologist skillset to identify a serial killer whose crimes are threaded to the case that established her career nearly 30 years prior. For the uninitiated: Get ready for Jamie Lee Curtis as Dorothy Farinelli, Scarpetta’s agent of chaos big sister. — Arno

entries.name: Undertone
entries.description: Nothing raises our blood pressure like the unknown, and this new A24 fright flick has quite a few of those. First off, the movie comes from first-time feature director Ian Tuason, whose skills look to have landed him a job heading up the next Paranormal Activity installment. Second, the cast is brimming with talented actors yet to become household names. And finally, there’s the story itself. Sure, we know Undertone is about a paranormal podcaster and some seriously disturbing audio files. But what do the recordings mean? And just how much horror are they about to unleash? We can’t know for sure until the film’s wide release this month. But one thing seems clear. Whatever’s on that tape wants to be heard. — Adrienne

entries.name: Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man
entries.description: England, 1940. It’s been six years since we last saw Tommy Shelby (Cillian Murphy), and Birmingham’s favorite gangster has walked away from the empire he built and the life he knew. That is, until he receives word that his son (Barry Keoghan) is wreaking havoc on Small Heath. The old Tommy must return and attempt to fix what's broken — in his hometown, in his family, and in himself. By order of the Peaky Blinders, he’s back. — Hillary

entries.name: Project Hail Mary
entries.description: Ryan Gosling? Check. Ryan Gosling doing cool science s—t in space? Check. Ryan Gosling becoming best friends with an alien while trying to save the universe? Checkkkk. If you haven't read the novel, you should. Better yet, listen to the audiobook narrated by master VO artist Ray Porter. Then, don't miss this film from the creative minds behind The Martian. You'll laugh, you'll cry, and you'll walk away with a whole new appreciation for your childhood science teachers. — Hillary

entries.name: Ready or Not 2: Here I Come
entries.description: Let the games begin again! Samara Weaving returns for Round 2 as Grace, a blushing bride-to-be who first discovered she was the sacrificial center of a sinister game back in 2019’s Ready or Not. Now, after surviving that harrowing night, she learns there is a new level to this nightmare. Can Grace outsmart the wealthy people sent to hunt her down and save her sister (Kathryn Newton) from an untimely death? Not if Elijah Wood, Sarah Michelle Gellar, Nestor Carbonell, Shawn Hatosy, and David Cronenberg have anything to say about it. May the best player win. — Adrienne

entries.name: Deadloch
entries.detail: Season 2 premieres March 20 on Prime Video
entries.description: While watching Deadloch’s first season three years ago I went from “what am I watching?” to “oh, it’s a satirical comedy,” then “I’m in love with this show.” The Australian buddy-cop comedy filled the “Kath & Kim”-sized hole in my heart, and I remember selling like a half dozen friends on it. Season 2 finds Detectives Dulcie Collins and Eddie Redcliffe in Darwin, situated in the country’s sweltering Northern Territory, where their latest case is derailed by a grim discovery. If you haven’t joined the cult yet, watch Season 1 now and pretend you’ve been a disciple for years. — Arno

entries.name: Something Very Bad Is Going to Happen
entries.description: With a title like that, at least you know what you’re getting into in this Netflix nuptial horror series. The idea of tying the knot can be scary enough without being reminded of Rosemary’s Baby or Carrie, but those horror classics were visual and tonal influences for showrunner and executive producer Haley Z. Boston. Starring Camila Morrone (“Daisy Jones & The Six,” “The Night Manager”) and Adam DiMarco (“The White Lotus,” “Overcompensating”) as the engaged couple, the eight-episode series depicts the tense week leading up to their almost-certainly doomed wedding. — James

entries.name: Alpha
entries.description: A movie-critic friend has prepped me a bit for what’s to come in Alpha. He and I came of age as young gay men during the HIV/AIDS crisis, and Alpha is viewed by many as writer-director Julia Ducournau’s grim metaphor for the epidemic. Her story is set in a decaying world, one consumed by generational trauma, where a teenage girl contracts a seemingly otherworldy virus after getting a tattoo. If you remember “greyscale” from “Game of Thrones,” think along those lines, but make it way more of a real-world possibility. — Arno

plot_text.plain_text: A darkly comedic tale of three middle-aged individuals entangled in a love triangle, leading to one's untimely demise.

Ex-Navy SEAL Kayce Dutton leaves Yellowstone Ranch to join U.S. Marshals, using his cowboy and military skills to fight crime in Montana while dealing with family ties and the mental toll of law enforcement.

A disgraced, young Sherlock Holmes finds himself wrapped up in a murder case that threatens his liberty. His first ever case unravels a globe-trotting conspiracy, culminating in an explosive showdown that changes his life forever.
A woman's life spirals as she develops an obsession with her alluring coworker. Her determination to make her fantasies real leads to dark, humorous, and complicated situations.
Follow the final recruits of a grueling special ops boot camp who encounter a deadly force from beyond this world.

node.text: The Bride! - Es lebe die Braut
The Bride! - Es lebe die Braut

plot_text.plain_text: In 1930s Chicago, Frankenstein asks Dr. Euphronius to help create a companion. They give life to a murdered woman as the Bride, sparking romance, police interest, and radical social change.
A 19-year-old criminal, Tommy, is kidnapped and forced into a rehabilitation process by a dysfunctional couple, Chris and Kathryn, who try to make him a "good boy." Tommy must find a way to escape.
A burned-out webtoon artist discovers a unique virtual dating subscription service that lets her meet her ideal matches, blurring the lines between her demanding reality and digital romance.
With his straw hat and ragtag crew, young pirate Monkey D. Luffy goes on an epic voyage for treasure.
A brilliant forensic pathologist uses forensic technology to solve crimes.
The host of a popular paranormal podcast becomes haunted by terrifying recordings mysteriously sent her way.

node.text: Bóng Ma Anh Quốc: Người Bất Tử

plot_text.plain_text: During World War II, Tommy Shelby returns to a bombed Birmingham and becomes involved in secret wartime missions based on true events, facing new threats as he reckons with his past and rising national stakes.

node.text: プロジェクト・ヘイル・メアリー

plot_text.plain_text: Science teacher Ryland Grace wakes up alone on a spaceship light-years from Earth. As his memory returns, he uncovers a mission to stop a mysterious substance killing the sun, and save Earth. An unexpected friendship may be the key.

plot_text.plain_text: After surviving one deadly game, Grace and her sister Faith must now outrun four rival families competing for a powerful throne - winner takes all.

plot_text.plain_text: Investigating murder and a rising body count Dulcie Collins and Eddie Radcliffe join forces with the ragtag team of misfits to bring a killer to justice.

plot_text.plain_text: Alpha, a troubled 13-year-old lives with her single mom. Their world collapses the day she returns from school with a tattoo on her arm.
